title: API Timeout on High Load — Azure Functions
description: After migrating to Azure Functions, we are seeing 30% of requests timing out above 500 concurrent users. This was flagged as a risk in MTG-001 but migration proceeded anyway.

comments.author: Ravi Sharma
comments.body: Confirmed this is still an issue as of today.

comments.author: Alex Johnson
comments.body: Root cause identified: insufficient load testing before migration.

title: PostgreSQL to MongoDB migration breaks reporting
description: The data migration has broken 14 existing SQL reporting queries. This was flagged by Ravi in EMAIL-003 but the risk was not addressed before go-live.

comments.author: Neha Gupta
comments.body: Working on a fix, ETA 2 days.

title: JWT refresh token vulnerability — Auth Service
description: As formally objected to in MTG-003, the lack of refresh token rotation is a security vulnerability. A penetration test confirmed the issue. Escalating to CTO.

comments.author: Michael Brown
comments.body: This was raised before we migrated. See EMAIL-003.

title: Vendor evaluation framework needs update
description: The Vendor X rejection highlighted gaps in our evaluation criteria. Need to add GDPR compliance and SLA requirements as mandatory fields.

comments.author: Pooja Verma
comments.body: Framework updated with new mandatory fields.

title: Azure vendor lock-in risk documentation
description: Per Ravi's concern raised in MTG-001, documenting the vendor lock-in risks of Azure Functions migration for future reference.

comments.author: Alex Johnson
comments.body: Risk documented in architecture decision records.
